Module: F - Governance, risk, and compliance awareness
Learning Outcome: Know that the Maintenance Policy and its sub-policies (Configuration Management, Change Enablement, Vulnerability & Patch Management) exist and are managed by IT; if you observe something that may be a maintenance issue or incident, raise a flag in Slack #general, DM an IT team member or your manager, or email security@civicactions.com

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Understand that complete CI inventories and baselines are maintained as code; know that configuration drift is detected, ticketed, and resolved; identify the Configuration Management Policy for detailed guidelines

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Classify production changes as Standard, Normal, Significant, or Emergency; know that peer review, automated tests, and progressive rollouts serve as approvals for low-risk changes; identify the Change Enablement Policy for detailed guidelines

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Understand the vulnerability lifecycle (discover, prioritize, remediate, verify); know that anti-malware protections are maintained with periodic and real-time scanning; identify the Vulnerability and Patch Management Policy for detailed guidelines

Explain that managed laptops are required for Internal/Confidential/client data with baseline controls (FDE, EDR, firewall, auto-lock, patching); understand that BYOD mobile is MFA/communications only with MDM profile isolation; know that non-compliant devices are blocked

Use only software from the Approved Software Catalog; identify the Prohibited Hardware and Software list; understand that MDM blocks unapproved software and that new software requests follow the access and authorization workflow

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Know that maintenance windows are published per service; understand that emergency changes follow IR communications; explain what change freezes are and when they may be applied

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Learning Outcome: Understand that change tickets, pipeline/scan results, CMDB entries, and metrics are Controlled Records; know the key program metrics (patch-SLA compliance, mean time to remediate, drift rate, emergency change volume, MDM violations, SBOM coverage)
